steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Count the exposed faces
steps.content: A rectangular box has 6 faces total. Since this box has no lid, the top face is missing. This leaves 5 exposed faces: 1 bottom and 4 side walls.

steps.title: Calculate the total exterior surface area
steps.content:
Each face is a square with side length 29 inches. The area of one square face is:
$$29^2 = 841\text{ square inches}$$
The total exterior surface area with 5 faces is:
$$5 \times 841 = 4205\text{ square inches}$$
